description: The phenomenon in which electric current is generated by varying magnetic fields.
embedding_text: Electromagnetic induction is the phenomenon where electric current is generated by varying magnetic fields. This chapter covers Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, motional electromotive force, inductance, and the principles of AC generators.
chunk_text: Electromagnetic induction is the phenomenon in which electric current is generated by varying magnetic fields. The pioneering experiments of Faraday and Henry led directly to the development of modern day generators and transformers.
subject: Physics

description: Historical context of the relationship between electricity and magnetism, highlighting the discoveries of Faraday and Henry.
embedding_text: Electricity and magnetism were once considered separate phenomena. Oersted and Ampere showed that moving electric charges produce magnetic fields. Later, Michael Faraday and Joseph Henry demonstrated the converse effect: moving magnets or changing magnetic fields can induce electric currents in closed coils.
chunk_text: Experiments by Oersted and Ampere established that moving electric charges produce magnetic fields. The experiments of Michael Faraday and Joseph Henry demonstrated conclusively that electric currents were induced in closed coils when subjected to changing magnetic fields.
subject: Physics

concept_title: The Experiments of Faraday and Henry
description: A series of foundational experiments demonstrating electromagnetic induction using magnets and coils.
embedding_text: Faraday and Henry conducted a series of experiments showing that relative motion between a magnet and a coil, or between two coils, induces an electric current. They also discovered that changing the current in a stationary coil induces a current in a neighboring stationary coil.
chunk_text: The discovery and understanding of electromagnetic induction are based on a long series of experiments carried out by Faraday and Henry. These experiments involved relative motion between a magnet and a coil, and between two coils.
subject: Physics

description: Definition and mathematical representation of magnetic flux through a surface.
embedding_text: Magnetic flux through a plane of area A in a uniform magnetic field B is the dot product of the magnetic field and the area vector. It is a scalar quantity measured in webers. For non-uniform fields, it is the surface integral of the magnetic field over the area elements. The figures show a plane surface in a uniform magnetic field and an arbitrary surface divided into area elements.
chunk_text: Magnetic flux through a plane of area A placed in a uniform magnetic field B is defined as the dot product of B and A. The SI unit of magnetic flux is weber (Wb) or tesla meter squared. Magnetic flux is a scalar quantity.
subject: Physics
